execution_changes: world-block preparation reused across fit seeds
separate resumable selection, fit, calibration, and confirmation checkpoints
atomic per-block metrics, trace shards, and completion markers

equivalence_contract.training_and_evaluation_features: bitwise equality against v1 on disjoint pilot worlds
equivalence_contract.calibration: same full and center-only bundles while reusing one model prediction
equivalence_contract.world_metrics: absolute tolerance 2e-12 only for scalar floating arithmetic
equivalence_contract.float_trace_fields: absolute tolerance 1e-5 for batched-versus-single-world float32 matrix multiplication; maximum randomized-pilot observation before freeze was 7.62939453125e-6
equivalence_contract.numpy_cpp_decoder: bitwise equality for all returned arrays on 10000 randomized rows
equivalence_contract.native_checks: release and unoptimized Debug native self-tests must pass
equivalence_contract.performance_gate: projected 5000-world, eight-seed confirmation evaluation must not exceed 900 seconds

native_kernel.ownership: all arrays are contiguous and caller-owned
semantic_responsibilities: accepted structured and center-only interval decoding
cross-entropy K3 decoding with exact evidence and polarity overrides
signed margins and rectified normalized margins

entry_point.ordering: a current passing repair pilot is required before selection; each later stage requires the preceding hashed checkpoint
entry_point.resumption: completed blocks are reused only after their metrics and every trace-shard hash verify
entry_point.final_guard: confirmation uses a distinct exact token and cannot begin before selection, fitting, and calibration checkpoints exist
